title: Easy Seitan
image_alt: Crispy seitan pieces tossed in sweet chili sauce with sesame seeds and green onions
source_label: Original recipe: It Doesn't Taste Like Chicken
steps: Add about an inch of water to a pot fitted with a steamer basket and bring to a light boil.

Whisk together the vital wheat gluten, flour, broth powder, onion powder, garlic powder, and salt. Pour in the water and mix until a dough ball forms. Lightly grease the steamer basket, tear off bite-sized pieces of dough, and place them in a single layer.
While the seitan steams, whisk together the sweet chili sauce, hoisin, soy sauce, water, rice vinegar, and sesame oil in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Using tongs, transfer the steamed seitan pieces to the hot oil. Fry for a few minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Remove from heat, pour the sauce over, and toss to coat. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions. Serve hot.
